info.title: Sleak Services API
info.description: Combined OpenAPI document for all Sleak FastAPI services. Operations are grouped by service tag; each path is prefixed with its service name so the services coexist in one document.

tags.name: Agent
tags.description: A LangGraph-based API for flexible agent configuration with optional tools

tags.name: Memory Service
tags.description: A service for managing conversation memory using PostgreSQL checkpointing

tags.name: Evaluations Processor
tags.description: A FastAPI application for processing chat evaluations

tags.name: Chat Evaluation Queue
tags.description: A FastAPI application for managing chat evaluation queues with 15-minute timeout

tags.name: Chat Queue (queueing_chat_closed)
tags.description: A FastAPI application for managing chat queues with configurable timeout

tags.name: Chat Queue (queueing_tags)
tags.description: A FastAPI application for managing chat queues with 15-minute timeout

tags.name: Cloud Run Job Execution Cleanup Service
tags.description: A FastAPI application for purging old Cloud Run job executions

post.description:
Receives an inbound visitor message, runs the LangGraph agent with the chatbot's configuration and tools, and posts the AI response back to the chat.

Actual path on the Agent service: `/webhook/message`.

Forwards the raw JSON body to the XML embedding processor, running it via the sleak-embedding-xml Hatchet workflow and falling back to the embedding-xml Cloud Run service. See the Embedding XML Processor service for the request body schema.

Actual path on the Sleak Gateway service: `/embedding_xml_processor`.

Forwards the raw JSON body to the web embedding queue, running it via the sleak-embedding-web-queue Hatchet workflow and falling back to the embedding-web-queue Cloud Run service. See the Embedding Web service for the request body schema.

Actual path on the Sleak Gateway service: `/embedding_web`.

Forwards the raw JSON body to the evaluation queueing service, running it via the sleak-queue-evaluation Hatchet workflow and falling back to the queue-evaluation Cloud Run service. See the Queueing Evaluations service for the request body schema.

Actual path on the Sleak Gateway service: `/queueing_evaluations`.

Forwards the raw JSON body to the evaluations processor, running it via the sleak-process-evaluation Hatchet workflow and falling back to the process-evaluation Cloud Run service. See the Evaluations Processor service for the request body schema.

Actual path on the Sleak Gateway service: `/evaluations_processor`.

Forwards the raw JSON body to the Agent service, running it via the sleak-agent-process-message Hatchet workflow (tagging chat_id, chatbot_id and organization_id as metadata) and falling back to the agent Cloud Run service at /webhook/message. See the Agent service for the request body schema.

Actual path on the Sleak Gateway service: `/agent`.

Accepts a multipart CSV upload plus datasource, organization and chatbot identifiers, then embeds it via the sleak-embedding-csv Hatchet workflow (base64-encoding the file) and falls back to the embedding-csv Cloud Run service on failure.

Actual path on the Sleak Gateway service: `/embedding/csv`.

Accepts a multipart PDF upload plus datasource, organization and chatbot identifiers, then embeds it via the sleak-embedding-pdf Hatchet workflow (base64-encoding the file) and falls back to the embedding-pdf Cloud Run service on failure.

Actual path on the Sleak Gateway service: `/embedding/pdf`.

get.description:
Returns a plain "OK" string confirming the gateway is running.

Actual path on the Sleak Gateway service: `/`.

post.description:
Appends the provided messages to a session's PostgreSQL-checkpointed conversation history, deduplicating any messages whose id already exists in the session and sorting new messages by their timestamp before persisting.

Actual path on the Memory Service service: `/add-messages`.

Alias of /embedding_web. Scrapes a datasource's web pages and embeds them, dispatching queued pages for v2 datasources or preparing a job from the supplied URLs, then offloading to Hatchet or Cloud Run.

Actual path on the Embedding Web service: `/process`.

Entry point for scraping a datasource's web pages and embedding them. For v2 datasources it dispatches the already-queued scrape_pages; otherwise it prepares a job from the supplied URLs (or the rescrape objects), then offloads to Hatchet or falls back to a Cloud Run job.

Actual path on the Embedding Web service: `/embedding_web`.

Re-discovers a v2 datasource's live sources and reconciles them against stored pages: detecting added, deleted, and (via sitemap lastmod) changed URLs, purging removed pages and their embeddings, and dispatching a job for the pages that need reprocessing.

Actual path on the Embedding Web service: `/recurring`.

Deletes all scrape_pages and their Zilliz embeddings that belong to a given source_url within a datasource. Validates that the source_url exists in the datasource_type_config before deleting.

Actual path on the Embedding Web service: `/sitemap/delete`.

schema.title: Response Sitemap Delete Endpoint Sitemap Delete Post

post.description:
Discovers the sitemaps and crawlable sources for a website URL, optionally applying include/exclude page rules, and returns the grouped sources and URLs without scraping them.

Actual path on the Embedding Web service: `/sitemap/discover`.

Fetches and parses a single sitemap URL, returning its source entry with the discovered URLs. Returns 422 for invalid sitemap content and 502 when the sitemap cannot be retrieved.

Actual path on the Embedding Web service: `/sitemap/fetch`.

Force-dispatches an already-prepared job directly to a Cloud Run job, bypassing Hatchet. Used as a fallback when Hatchet offload is unavailable or a queued job needs to be run on Cloud Run.

Actual path on the Embedding Web service: `/embedding_web/cloud_run_fallback`.

schema.title: Response Cloud Run Fallback Endpoint Embedding Web Cloud Run Fallback Post

get.description:
Debug endpoint that lists Hatchet workers and reports the active job-serving workers along with their available and total slot counts. Returns hatchet_offload false when offload is disabled.

Actual path on the Embedding Web service: `/debug/hatchet_workers`.

post.description:
Runs LangSmith trace and thread evaluations for a chat and pushes the results to Grafana.

Actual path on the Evaluations Processor service: `/process`.

Queues a background QA evaluation for a single bot message, using the chat_id and message id from the provided record.

Actual path on the Evaluations Processor service: `/process_qa`.

Schedules a delayed Cloud Tasks job that triggers the n8n evaluation webhook for a chat. Skips queueing when the chatbot's daily rate limit is reached, the chat is already evaluated, the chat has no llm_model, or the chat has 5 or fewer messages; any existing task for the same chat is replaced.

Actual path on the Chat Evaluation Queue service: `/queueing/evaluations`.

Deletes the chat when 'delete' is true, otherwise schedules a delayed Cloud Tasks job to close it.

Actual path on the Chat Queue service: `/queueing/close_chat`.

schema.title: Response Queue Or Delete Chat Queueing Close Chat Post

post.description:
Schedules a Cloud Tasks job that calls the n8n webhook to tag the chat 15 minutes later. Any pending task previously queued for the same chat is deleted first, so the timer effectively resets on every call.

Actual path on the Chat Queue service: `/queueing/tags`.

Lists all Cloud Run jobs in the configured GCP project and region and deletes those whose executions have completed. When the job count reaches the internal threshold it deletes all completed jobs, otherwise it deletes only completed jobs older than the 7-day retention window. Requires the GCP_PROJECT_ID and GCP_REGION environment variables.

Actual path on the Cloud Run Job Execution Cleanup Service service: `/purge_jobs`.
tags: Cloud Run Job Execution Cleanup Service

chat_id.title: Chat Id
chat_id.description: Identifier of the chat the message belongs to.

visitor_id.title: Visitor Id
visitor_id.description: Identifier of the visitor who sent the message.

chatbot_id.title: Chatbot Id
chatbot_id.description: Identifier of the chatbot handling the conversation.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: Optional list of incoming messages for a multi-message turn.

channel.title: Channel
channel.description: Channel the message arrived on (e.g. chat-ui, whatsapp).

external_id.title: External Id
external_id.description: External system identifier for the message or visitor.

current_page.title: Current Page
current_page.description: URL of the page the visitor is currently viewing.

file.title: File
file.description: URL of a file attached to the message, if any.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Conversation thread identifier used as the checkpointer thread_id to scope stored messages

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: Array of message objects with role and content

status.title: Status
status.description: Outcome of the append operation; 'success' when the request was processed

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Conversation thread identifier the messages were appended to

messages_appended.title: Messages Appended
messages_appended.description: Number of messages received in the request payload, before deduplication

messages_ingested.title: Messages Ingested
messages_ingested.description: Number of new messages actually persisted after removing duplicates by id

total_messages.title: Total Messages
total_messages.description: Total number of messages stored in the session after this operation

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Optional Unix timestamp of the message

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: ID of an already-prepared job to (re)dispatch to a Cloud Run job.

organization_id.title: Organization Id
organization_id.description: Organization that owns the job; passed into the Cloud Run job env.

datasource_id.title: Datasource Id
datasource_id.description: ID of the v2 data_source to reconcile against its live sources.

urls.title: Urls
urls.description: URLs to scrape. For a fresh scrape this is a list of URL strings; when rescrape is true each item is an object carrying both 'url' and the existing 'id' of the scrape_pages row to update. Ignored for v2 datasources, which pull queued pages from the database instead.

datasource_id.title: Datasource Id
datasource_id.description: ID of the data_source these URLs belong to.

organization_id.title: Organization Id
organization_id.description: Organization that owns the job; if omitted it is inferred from the queued scrape_pages rows (v2).

chatbot_id.title: Chatbot Id
chatbot_id.description: Chatbot the scraped content is associated with; if omitted it is inferred from the queued scrape_pages rows (v2).

rescrape.title: Rescrape
rescrape.description: When true, treat each urls item as an existing page object ({'url', 'id'}) and re-scrape it in place rather than creating new pages.

success.title: Success
success.description: Whether the scrape job was accepted and dispatched.

message.title: Message
message.description: Human-readable summary of what was dispatched or why it was not.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error detail when the request could not be processed.

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: Identifier of the created job, used to track processing progress.

datasource_id.title: Datasource Id
datasource_id.description: ID of the data_source whose pages should be removed.

source_url.title: Source Url
source_url.description: The source_url (sitemap/crawl source) whose pages and embeddings should be deleted; must exist in the datasource_type_config sources list.

chat_id.title: Chat Id
chat_id.description: Unique identifier for the chat to evaluate.

record.description: Record containing the chat_id and bot message id to evaluate.

chat_id.title: Chat Id
chat_id.description: Identifier of the chat the QA pair belongs to.

id.title: Id
id.description: Identifier of the bot message being evaluated.

status.title: Status
status.description: Outcome of the queueing request: 'success' when a task was created, or 'skipped' when the chat was rate-limited, already evaluated, missing an llm_model, or had 5 or fewer messages

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Full Cloud Tasks resource name of the created evaluation task, or an empty string when the request was skipped

chat_id.title: Chat Id
chat_id.description: Identifier of the chat this response refers to, echoed back from the request

status.title: Status
status.description: Service health indicator, always 'healthy' when the endpoint responds

status.title: Status
status.description: Outcome of scheduling the close task.

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Identifier of the scheduled Cloud Tasks job.

id.title: Id
id.description: Identifier of the chat to close or delete.

delete.title: Delete
delete.description: If true, delete the chat instead of scheduling a close task.

visitor_id.title: Visitor Id
visitor_id.description: Identifier of the visitor associated with the chat.

chatbot_id.title: Chatbot Id
chatbot_id.description: Identifier of the chatbot handling the chat.

deleted_count.title: Deleted Count
deleted_count.description: Number of records deleted for the chat.

status.title: Status
status.description: Outcome of the queueing operation; 'success' when the task was created

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Fully qualified Cloud Tasks resource name of the created task

chat_id.title: Chat Id
chat_id.description: Identifier of the chat the queued task belongs to

status.title: Status
status.description: Service health indicator; 'healthy' when the API is up

status.title: Status
status.description: Service liveness indicator; returns 'healthy' when the application is running.

status.title: Status
status.description: Overall outcome of the purge run: 'success' when no errors occurred, 'partial_success' when some jobs or executions failed, or 'no_jobs_found' when no jobs existed in the region.

deleted_count.title: Deleted Count
deleted_count.description: Number of Cloud Run jobs for which a deletion was successfully initiated.

deleted_jobs.title: Deleted Jobs
deleted_jobs.description: Fully qualified resource names of the Cloud Run jobs that were deleted.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Human-readable error messages for jobs or executions that could not be listed or deleted.

total_jobs.title: Total Jobs
total_jobs.description: Total number of jobs discovered in the region and considered as deletion candidates.

purge_mode.title: Purge Mode
purge_mode.description: Strategy used for this run: 'threshold_purge' deletes all completed jobs once the job count reaches the threshold, otherwise 'age_purge' deletes only completed jobs older than the retention window.
